title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false
input_row.rationale: Profiling allows for the identification of specific performance bottlenecks. It generates a significant amount of program data that could potentially be exploited to uncover system and program details. If you are not experiencing any bottlenecks and do not need the profiler for troubleshooting purposes, it is recommended to turn it off to reduce the potential attack surface.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager Verify that the --profiling argument is set to false .
input_row.remediation: Edit the Controller Manager pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- controller-manager.yaml on the master node and set the below parameter. --profiling=false
input_row.impact: Profiling information would not be available.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kube_controller_manager_profiling_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the kube-controller-manager.
- The specific command to retrieve the kube-controller-manager pod configuration is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o jsonpath='{.items[?(@.spec.containers[*].name=="kube-controller-manager")].spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- This command will return the arguments passed to the kube-controller-manager container.
- The automated check will specifically look for the `--profiling=false` argument in the output.
- **Pass Criteria:** The output must include `--profiling=false`.
- **Fail Criteria:** The output does not include `--profiling=false` or includes `--profiling=true`.
